text.title: hybrid-search-implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for hybrid-search-implementation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Alpha tuning (dense vs sparse weight) is data-dependent; wrong alpha degrades results worse than single-method search — must be measured on a labeled eval set.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Alpha is corpus- and query-set-dependent. Generic defaults almost never match production data.

failure-mode.description: BM25 tokenization is language-specific; the default English tokenizer produces poor scores for CJK, Arabic, or other non-whitespace-delimited languages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Whitespace tokenization produces single-character or meaningless tokens for CJK text, leading to near-zero BM25 scores. Use a language-appropriate tokenizer.

failure-mode.description: RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) discards absolute scores, which can surface low-confidence results from both methods at the same rank.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: RRF normalizes all documents to their rank position. A document ranked 5th in both methods gets the same RRF score as one that is clearly top-1 in both. Apply a minimum per-method score filter before fusion.

failure-mode.description: Pinecone's sparse-dense hybrid requires training BM25Encoder on the full corpus before ingestion; corpus size changes invalidate the encoder.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BM25Encoder learns corpus-level IDF statistics. If the corpus changes, the encoder's IDF weights become stale and must be retrained.

failure-mode.description: Qdrant's native hybrid (prefetch + FusionQuery) requires sparse vectors to be pre-computed outside Qdrant — there is no built-in BM25 tokenizer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Qdrant stores sparse vectors as (indices, values) tuples but does not tokenize text. Tokenization must happen in application code.

failure-mode.description: Elasticsearch hybrid scoring is sensitive to BM25 score scale vs cosine similarity scale; naive linear combination requires per-corpus normalization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BM25 scores are unbounded and corpus-dependent. Cosine similarity is bounded [0, 1]. Directly adding them produces meaningless results. Normalize BM25 scores before combination.

failure-mode.description: Agents must fit the BM25Encoder on the full corpus before any ingestion — fitting after partial ingestion produces incorrect IDF weights.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fitting on a partial corpus produces IDF weights that are stale once the corpus grows. This requires retraining.

failure-mode.description: Pinecone sparse vector format is {"indices": [...], "values": [...]} — agents must not confuse it with Qdrant's SparseVector(indices=..., values=...).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The two APIs use different Python types and conventions. Mixing them produces type errors.

failure-mode.description: Weaviate's alpha=0 is pure BM25, alpha=1 is pure vector — the opposite convention from some papers where alpha=0 means full semantic; always verify the client's convention.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Conventions vary. Verify the client library's convention before shipping.

failure-mode.description: RRF k=60 is a heuristic that works well for document search; for code search with hundreds of results, k=30 may be better — agents should expose k as a config param.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The optimal k value depends on the result set size and domain.

failure-mode.description: Multi-language corpora need per-language tokenizers in BM25; a single English BM25 tokenizer on a mixed corpus degrades non-English recall.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Running two retrieval paths has additive latency in serial mode; agents should use asyncio.gather or thread-pool to parallelize dense and sparse searches.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Parallelization reduces P99 latency vs serial execution.
